title: × dactylocamptis weberi
intro: × Dactylocamptis weberi, also known as the Weber's winter aconite, is a rare and endangered orchid species native to the Mediterranean region. It is a hybrid of Dactylorhiza and Campanula orchids, characterized by its yellow flowers and unique growth habit. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of × Dactylocamptis weberi include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and potentially treating bacterial infections, with herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves being used to harness its medicinal properties.
